title: Need some help for a paper
display_title: Need some help for a paper
wikitext:
[]: Need some help for a paper             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Nov 08, 2006 2:05 pm I'm not sure if this is the right place, but... I need some help for a paper I'm writing on Hideaki Anno and Hayao Miyazaki, and they were rejecting what anime was when Anno made Eva, and Miyazaki made Nausicaa. Can anybody suggest what anime around the time of those that they feel it would be best to compare? Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Nov 08, 2006 2:59 pm Gundam is an obvious comparison, as the most enduring (non-super) robot anime. Checking Wikipedia, I see that Wing was the series closest to a contemporary (ho ho). Sailor Moon is another well known series that spanned the same period. From my collection, the only title from the same period is Dirty Pair Flash, which is fairly niche by comparison.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Wed Nov 08, 2006 4:47 pm Actually a lot of what Miyazaki created was based off of early anime like The Adventures of Hols, Prince of the Sun by Isao Takahata so he wasn't flat out rejecting what everyone else was doing just building on a type of anime created by Takahata. As for other anime out around the mid eighties the only ones I can think of are Dirty Pair, Fist of the North Star, and the original Dragon Ball. As for the mid nineties when Eva came out you got Gundam, Kenshin, YuYu Hakusho, Escaflowne, and Sailor Moon. Thatâ€™s all I can think of for now.

[]: Re: Need some help for a paper             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Nov 08, 2006 5:09 pm cat42 wrote: I'm not sure if this is the right place, but... I need some help for a paper I'm writing on Hideaki Anno and Hayao Miyazaki, and they were rejecting what anime was when Anno made Eva, and Miyazaki made Nausicaa. Can anybody suggest what anime around the time of those that they feel it would be best to compare? Thanks.

[]: Re: Need some help for a paper             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Nov 08, 2006 6:12 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I'm confused by your post, but what I think you're referring to is something that relates to Laputa/Nadia.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Nov 08, 2006 9:07 pm Anno, like most of the men of Gainax, wanted to make anime for anime fans. Miyazaki doesn't make "anime", he makes "animated works". He doesn't like being put within that genre, and I don't blame him. He also dislikes foreigners, particularly Americans. Hence why he (supposedly) failed to appear to accept his academy award for Spirited Away.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Thu Nov 09, 2006 10:44 am I don't really think Annos attitude towards this has changed all that much until maybe really recently, I remember reading that when he first saw the opening for Re: Cutie Honey done by Imaishi he said something to the effect of that for the first time in years he had hope for the future of anime.
